notes: Reproduced data corruption in Blob.FromStream on all tested versions (HarfBuzzSharp 7.3.0.3, 8.3.1.3, and main source). The fixed block at Blob.cs:79 pins the managed byte array only during the block scope, but the pointer escapes to native code via hb_blob_create. After the block exits, GC compaction relocates the array, leaving a dangling pointer. Reproduction shows 3/200 iterations with corrupt data (offset 1: expected 0x01, got 0x00). This is a universal managed memory safety bug — not platform-specific. Skipped Phase 3D cross-platform since this is a pure managed heap issue.

corrections.corrected: area/HarfBuzzSharp — the bug is in HarfBuzzSharp.Blob.FromStream, not SkiaSharp

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the detailed analysis! We've confirmed this GC safety issue.

**Reproduction results:**

| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| HarfBuzzSharp 7.3.0.3 | ❌ Data corruption (3/200 iterations) |
| HarfBuzzSharp 8.3.1.3 (latest) | ❌ Data corruption (3/200 iterations) |
| main (source) | ❌ Data corruption (3/200 iterations) |

The `fixed` block at `Blob.cs:79` pins the managed byte array only for the duration of the block, but the pointer escapes into native code via `hb_blob_create`. After the block exits, GC compaction can relocate the array, leaving the native blob with a dangling pointer.

Our repro creates a 64KB blob from a stream with a known byte pattern, forces GC compaction, then reads the data back — corruption is detected at a rate of ~1.5% of iterations.

PR #3473 addresses this with `Marshal.AllocCoTaskMem`, which matches the established pattern used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g., `SKImage.cs`, `SKFrontBufferedManagedStream.cs`).

actionability.reason: GC safety bug confirmed on all versions including main. The fixed block at Blob.cs:79 lets a pointer escape to native code. PR #3473 proposes a fix. Bug manifests as data corruption (3/200 iterations) which could cause intermittent crashes in production.

workarounds: Use Blob.FromFile() instead of Blob.FromStream() when loading from files, as hb_blob_create_from_file handles its own memory allocation
Manually allocate unmanaged memory with Marshal.AllocCoTaskMem, copy stream data to it, and construct the Blob with a release delegate that calls Marshal.FreeCoTaskMem

actions.description: Fix area label to HarfBuzzSharp (bug is in HarfBuzzSharp.Blob, not SkiaSharp) and add os/All since this is cross-platform
Link to PR #3473 which proposes a fix using Marshal.AllocCoTaskMem
